chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ________________________________________________________________ Date Order with Signature of Judge ________________________________________________________________ C.P. No.S-546 of 2012 United Bank Limited.............................................................Petitioner Versus M/s. Habib Commercial Co Ltd & others..................................................Respondents C.P. No.S-548 of 2012 United Bank Limited...............................................................Petitioner Versus M/s. Sattar & Sattar Ltd & others........................................................Respondents Date of hearing: 16.05.2025 Date of judgment: 16.05.2025 Mr. Abdul Haleem Siddiqui, Advocate for the Petitioners. Mr. Haider Imam Rizvi, Advocate for Respondent No.1 in C.P.No.S-546/2012. Mr. Naeem Suleman, Advocate for Respondent No.1 in C.P.No.S- 548/2012. ----------------------- J U D G E M E N T By consent, both the judgments of the Courts below are set-aside and the matter is remanded back to the learned Rent Controller in light of the question already framed vide order dated 30.04.2025, in the following terms: - i. The learned Rent Controller shall hear and decide the rent application within thirty days starting from 17.05.2025 (excluding the summer vacations). ii. The Petitioner shall file additional Affidavit-in-evidence before the learned Rent Controller, reiterating personal bonafide need. The opponent in the Rent Case shall be at liberty to cross examine on the contents of additional Affidavit only. 2 iii. The learned Rent Controller while adjudicating the rent application shall consider the evidence already recorded in addition to the additional affidavit and the relevant cross examination thereon as noted above. iv.

The opponent in the Rent Case shall be at liberty to cross examine on the contents of additional Affidavit only. 2 iii. The learned Rent Controller while adjudicating the rent application shall consider the evidence already recorded in addition to the additional affidavit and the relevant cross examination thereon as noted above. iv. The learned Rent Controller shall adjudicate the case on merits and shall only determine Issue No.2 and 3. v. It is specified that this matter is pending since 1993 and no further delay is warranted. If circumstances warrant, the learned Rent Controller can proceed with the matter on day-to-day basis and furnish report to the MIT II of this Court on or before the expiry of the above-mentioned period. Both the petitions stand disposed of in the above terms along with all pending application(s) Judge Nadeem
